sections.paragraph:
pr.n.-

(1) Amalek, the Amalekites, a very ancient people ( Gen 14:7 Numb. 24:20 ), inhabiting the regions south of Palestine, between Edom and Egypt (compare -Ex. 17:816 ; Numb. 13:30 1Sa 15:7), also dwelling on the east of the Dead Sea and Mount Seir ( Num 24:20 Jud. 3:13 6:3 , 33 Jdg 6:33); they seem also to have settled here and there in the middle of Canaan, whence the Mount of the Amalekites in the tribe of Ephraim, Jdg 12:15 compare Jdg 5:14.-In the Arabian genealogies عِمْلِيقُ, عَمَالِيقُ is mentioned amongst the aboriginal Arabians. See Relandi Palæstina, p. 78-82; J. D. Michaëlis, Spicileg. Geogr. Hebr. Ext. tom. i. p. 170-177; ejusd. Supplemm. p. 1927; Vater, Comment. über den Pentat. vol. i. p. 140; and my remarks in Ersch and Gruber’s Encycl. iii. 301, under the word Amalek.-Gent. noun עֲמָלֵקִי, with the art. collect. Gen 14:7 Jud. 12:15 .

(2) a grandson of Esau, and the founder of an Arabian tribe, Gen 36:16 compare verse 12 Gen 36:12and Vater, loc. cit.
